commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer proof collection or explicit proof override; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.; Cleared: The diff only changes Telegram dispatch logic and colocated tests; it does not touch dependencies, workflows, package metadata, secrets, or code-download surfaces. (sha=02b189044f35852feb90714d2ad7f27f71d2704a)
commands.review_summary:
The PR updates Telegram message dispatch to suppress text-only post-final tool and tagged non-terminal warning payloads while preserving terminal final errors, media, exec approvals, with regression tests.

PR surface: Source +10, Tests +68. Total +78 across 2 files.

Reproducibility: no. high-confidence live reproduction is attached. Source-level fixtures reproduce the final-then-late-payload sequence, but there is no current-head Telegram run showing the user-visible behavior.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining action is maintainer proof collection or explicit proof override; there is no narrow automated code repair to queue.

target.title: fix(telegram): suppress post-final tool error noise
target.body:
## Summary
- Suppress text-only tool/progress noise once a Telegram final answer has started or delivered
- Preserve terminal `final` error warnings, media payloads, and exec approval prompts
- Suppress tagged `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning` finals only after a final answer has actually delivered
- Add regression coverage for late tool noise suppression, final warning preservation, tagged post-final warning suppression, and first-warning preservation

## Why
Agents often run exploratory commands/searches where a failure or empty result is expected, such as file searches that return no matches. The model can handle that internally and still produce a correct final answer. If those handled tool errors are delivered after the final reply, they are non-actionable noise for the user and make a successful turn look broken.

At the same time, core intentionally emits terminal `final` `isError` warnings for mutating write/exec failures when the assistant claimed success. Those must stay visible. This PR preserves those warnings and only drops post-final tool/progress noise or final warnings explicitly tagged by core as non-terminal after a user-facing final has delivered.

## Due diligence
- Reviewed ClawSweeper/Codex P1 feedback on preserving post-final error finals
- Repaired the broad `payload.isError` suppression so terminal `final` warnings remain visible
- Kept the existing core mutating-tool warning contract intact
- Keyed final-error suppression to existing reply metadata: `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning`
- Reviewed Codex P2 feedback on `finalAnswerDeliveryStarted` vs `finalAnswerDelivered`
- Gated tagged warning-final suppression on `finalAnswerDelivered`, while leaving tool-kind post-final suppression keyed to started or delivered final state
- Added regression coverage so a tagged non-terminal warning final remains visible when no final reply was delivered yet
- Latest ClawSweeper review indicates no narrow automated code repair is needed; remaining blocker is current-head live Telegram proof or maintainer proof override
- Rebased onto `origin/main` `099584676b` and squashed into single commit `02b189044f`

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior addressed: Telegram should hide non-actionable post-final tool/progress failures, while preserving terminal final write/exec warnings, media payloads, exec approval prompts, and tagged warning finals when no user-facing final reply has been delivered yet.
- Real environment tested: Live OpenClaw Telegram runtime in the `Openclaw debug` Telegram topic after maintainer/operator build and restart on an earlier squashed head.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Keshav G confirmed from the live Telegram debug topic on 2026-06-03 13:06:19 IST / 07:36:19 UTC that the OpenClaw runtime was built and restarted after the PR was rebased and squashed to then-current head `1a59a77093f9`. On 2026-06-05, this branch was rebased again onto `origin/main` `099584676b` and force-pushed as current head `02b189044f` with the same one-commit PR surface plus the Codex P2 repair.
- Evidence after fix: Maintainer/operator confirmation was received in live Telegram: `Built and restarted` for head `1a59a77093f9`. Current local branch is `fix/suppress-post-final-tool-errors` at `02b189044f`, matching the PR head after the latest rebase and P2 repair.
- Observed result after fix: The live Telegram runtime came back online after the 2026-06-03 restart. The code P1 remains resolved; the Codex P2 has been addressed in current head `02b189044f`; the remaining ClawSweeper blocker is live Telegram behavior proof acceptance.
- What was not tested: I did not run a build, test, restart, Mantis desktop proof, or new live Telegram behavior probe after the 2026-06-05 rebase to `02b189044f`, per operator instruction to ask before build/test/restart. The existing live proof does not include a current-head Telegram transcript, recording, redacted runtime log, or artifact showing post-final non-terminal warning suppression and terminal warning preservation.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is maintainer/operator live Telegram confirmation from an older head, not a current-head Mantis bot artifact. The earlier Mantis Telegram proof request could not run from this contributor account because `keshavbotagent` has read-only repository permission. A maintainer-triggered Mantis Telegram Desktop proof, equivalent redacted live Telegram artifact, or explicit maintainer proof override is still needed before ClawSweeper can clear the proof gate. The failed Mantis authorization log was:

```text
Actor keshavbotagent permission: read
Workflow requires write/maintain/admin access. Actor "keshavbotagent" has "read".
Resolve Mantis request: skipped
Run agentic native Telegram proof: skipped
```

## Verification
Previous local verification before the rebase/squash:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
- `git diff --check`

Post-squash maintainer/operator confirmation:
- Built and restarted live OpenClaw Telegram runtime on 2026-06-03 13:06:19 IST / 07:36:19 UTC.

Post-2026-06-04 Codex P2 repair:
- The commit hook formatted the two touched files during `git commit --amend`.
- No build, test, or restart was run after this repair because operator approval is required first.

Post-2026-06-05 sync:
- Rebased onto `origin/main` `099584676b` and pushed current head `02b189044f`.
- No build, test, restart, Mantis proof, or live Telegram behavior probe was run after this sync because operator approval is required first.

## Current review state
- Code P1 is folded into current squashed head `02b189044f`
- Codex P2 is addressed in current head `02b189044f`
- Latest Codex follow-up found no major issues before this latest rebase; fresh review is requested again for current head
- Latest ClawSweeper review still requires stronger current-head real Telegram behavior proof
- Mantis proof request was attempted, but skipped due contributor permission
- Waiting on maintainer-triggered Mantis, equivalent live Telegram artifact, or explicit maintainer proof override before merge

commands.author: keshavbotagent

target.title: fix(telegram): suppress post-final tool error noise
target.body:
## Summary
- Suppress text-only tool/progress noise once a Telegram final answer has started or delivered
- Preserve terminal `final` error warnings, media payloads, and exec approval prompts
- Suppress tagged `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning` finals only after a final answer has actually delivered
- Add regression coverage for late tool noise suppression, final warning preservation, tagged post-final warning suppression, and first-warning preservation

## Why
Agents often run exploratory commands/searches where a failure or empty result is expected, such as file searches that return no matches. The model can handle that internally and still produce a correct final answer. If those handled tool errors are delivered after the final reply, they are non-actionable noise for the user and make a successful turn look broken.

At the same time, core intentionally emits terminal `final` `isError` warnings for mutating write/exec failures when the assistant claimed success. Those must stay visible. This PR preserves those warnings and only drops post-final tool/progress noise or final warnings explicitly tagged by core as non-terminal after a user-facing final has delivered.

## Due diligence
- Reviewed ClawSweeper/Codex P1 feedback on preserving post-final error finals
- Repaired the broad `payload.isError` suppression so terminal `final` warnings remain visible
- Kept the existing core mutating-tool warning contract intact
- Keyed final-error suppression to existing reply metadata: `nonTerminalToolErrorWarning`
- Reviewed Codex P2 feedback on `finalAnswerDeliveryStarted` vs `finalAnswerDelivered`
- Gated tagged warning-final suppression on `finalAnswerDelivered`, while leaving tool-kind post-final suppression keyed to started or delivered final state
- Added regression coverage so a tagged non-terminal warning final remains visible when no final reply was delivered yet
- Latest ClawSweeper review indicates no narrow automated code repair is needed; remaining blocker is current-head live Telegram proof or maintainer proof override
- Rebased onto `origin/main` `099584676b` and squashed into single commit `02b189044f`

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)
- Behavior addressed: Telegram should hide non-actionable post-final tool/progress failures, while preserving terminal final write/exec warnings, media payloads, exec approval prompts, and tagged warning finals when no user-facing final reply has been delivered yet.
- Real environment tested: Live OpenClaw Telegram runtime in the `Openclaw debug` Telegram topic after maintainer/operator build and restart on an earlier squashed head.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: Keshav G confirmed from the live Telegram debug topic on 2026-06-03 13:06:19 IST / 07:36:19 UTC that the OpenClaw runtime was built and restarted after the PR was rebased and squashed to then-current head `1a59a77093f9`. On 2026-06-05, this branch was rebased again onto `origin/main` `099584676b` and force-pushed as current head `02b189044f` with the same one-commit PR surface plus the Codex P2 repair.
- Evidence after fix: Maintainer/operator confirmation was received in live Telegram: `Built and restarted` for head `1a59a77093f9`. Current local branch is `fix/suppress-post-final-tool-errors` at `02b189044f`, matching the PR head after the latest rebase and P2 repair.
- Observed result after fix: The live Telegram runtime came back online after the 2026-06-03 restart. The code P1 remains resolved; the Codex P2 has been addressed in current head `02b189044f`; the remaining ClawSweeper blocker is live Telegram behavior proof acceptance.
- What was not tested: I did not run a build, test, restart, Mantis desktop proof, or new live Telegram behavior probe after the 2026-06-05 rebase to `02b189044f`, per operator instruction to ask before build/test/restart. The existing live proof does not include a current-head Telegram transcript, recording, redacted runtime log, or artifact showing post-final non-terminal warning suppression and terminal warning preservation.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This is maintainer/operator live Telegram confirmation from an older head, not a current-head Mantis bot artifact. The earlier Mantis Telegram proof request could not run from this contributor account because `keshavbotagent` has read-only repository permission. A maintainer-triggered Mantis Telegram Desktop proof, equivalent redacted live Telegram artifact, or explicit maintainer proof override is still needed before ClawSweeper can clear the proof gate. The failed Mantis authorization log was:

```text
Actor keshavbotagent permission: read
Workflow requires write/maintain/admin access. Actor "keshavbotagent" has "read".
Resolve Mantis request: skipped
Run agentic native Telegram proof: skipped
```

## Verification
Previous local verification before the rebase/squash:
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/payloads.errors.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p tsconfig.core.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core.tsbuildinfo`
